Description: Establish project management, documentation, validation, and contribution foundations.
Acceptance Criteria: Vision, roadmap, Jira backlog, CI, and workflow docs exist.

Description: Make the technology graph navigable, queryable, and useful for target-oriented planning.
Acceptance Criteria: Users can explore the graph, find dependencies, and identify paths to target technologies.

Description: Make each technology page a deep structured manual, not a shallow wiki article.
Acceptance Criteria: Entries support principles, materials, steps, failure modes, maintenance, sources, and verification.

Description: Map raw materials, Earth locations, space substitutes, and material transformation chains.
Acceptance Criteria: Users can search by material and see where it appears in technologies and chains.

Description: Make risk, uncertainty, source quality, and review status explicit and enforceable.
Acceptance Criteria: High-risk content is gated, labeled, source-backed, and validated.

Description: Adapt the same tree to collapse recovery, Mars, Moon, orbital station, and other scenarios.
Acceptance Criteria: Users can switch context and see priorities, warnings, and substitutions change.

Description: Add diagrams, images, videos, printable guides, and field cards.
Acceptance Criteria: Entries become teachable and printable without losing source/license tracking.

Description: Create pathways for experts and contributors to improve the project safely.
Acceptance Criteria: Jira/GitHub templates, missions, review queues, and contributor guidance exist.

Description: Ensure CivilizationX works as a static/offline artifact.
Acceptance Criteria: The PWA, static build, printable pages, and mirror strategy support low-connectivity use.

Description: Document the full CivilizationX plan and idea backlog in the repository.
Acceptance Criteria: docs/MASTER_PLAN.md exists and covers product, content, safety, media, community, and infrastructure.

Description: Create Jira-ready CSV and workflow docs.
Acceptance Criteria: docs/jira contains README, workflow, sprint plan, and CSV backlog.

Description: Run validation, lint, and build in CI.
Acceptance Criteria: GitHub Actions workflow exists and runs npm ci, validate-data, lint, and build.

Description: Resolve whether public name is CivilizationX, The Codex, or a combined brand.
Acceptance Criteria: Decision recorded in docs and README updated.

Description: Select appropriate licenses for code and curated content.
Acceptance Criteria: LICENSE files added and README updated.

Description: Define tone, source standards, uncertainty language, and safety phrasing.
Acceptance Criteria: docs includes style guide linked from CONTRIBUTING.md.

Description: Create a page for the primitive stack shared by collapse recovery and space settlement.
Acceptance Criteria: /foundations route exists and links core foundation nodes and chains.

Description: Let users choose a starting capability and a target technology.
Acceptance Criteria: Pathfinder returns prerequisite path and missing nodes.

Description: On technology pages, show missing upstream prerequisites for a selected capability set.
Acceptance Criteria: Panel lists required missing nodes and links to them.

Description: Show all prerequisites upstream of a selected node.
Acceptance Criteria: A selected technology can show its dependency subtree.

Description: Show all technologies unlocked downstream of a selected node.
Acceptance Criteria: A selected technology can show downstream unlocks.

Description: Identify nodes that unlock many downstream capabilities.
Acceptance Criteria: Status or tree view highlights high-leverage nodes.

Description: Prevent impossible cyclic dependencies.
Acceptance Criteria: validate-data fails on cycles and reports cycle path.

Description: Detect isolated nodes with no prerequisites and no unlocks unless explicitly allowed.
Acceptance Criteria: validate-data reports orphan candidates.

Description: Support technologies with multiple possible prerequisite routes.
Acceptance Criteria: Schema design supports required and alternative prerequisites.

Description: Explain why each prerequisite is needed.
Acceptance Criteria: Schema and UI support prerequisite rationale.

Description: Track stub, draft, researched, community-reviewed, and expert-verified maturity.
Acceptance Criteria: Schema/UI display maturity separately from verification.

Description: Every technology should explain how it fails and dangerous mistakes.
Acceptance Criteria: Schema and UI support failureModes.

Description: Document how to keep technologies working.
Acceptance Criteria: Schema and UI support maintenance.

Description: Track what each technology consumes and produces.
Acceptance Criteria: Schema supports inputs, outputs, and byproducts.

Description: Document waste streams and disposal concerns.
Acceptance Criteria: Schema validates wasteHandling for high-risk entries.

Description: Separate human skills from tools and materials.
Acceptance Criteria: Entries can list required skills.

Description: Track rough time ranges for building or learning.
Acceptance Criteria: Entries can list setup, build, cure, grow, or training times.

Description: Classify household, workshop, village, industrial, national, habitat scale.
Acceptance Criteria: Entry pages show scale.

Description: Track whether rough craft or high precision is required.
Acceptance Criteria: Schema and UI expose precision level.

Description: Track heat and pressure constraints for processes.
Acceptance Criteria: Validator can flag missing warnings for high temperature or pressure.

Description: Define the first 100 nodes that matter most.
Acceptance Criteria: docs lists founding 100 with priority and status.

Description: Deepen primitive nodes before expanding advanced branches.
Acceptance Criteria: Foundation nodes have failure modes, maintenance, and diagrams planned.

Description: Normalize raw materials across entries.
Acceptance Criteria: Schema supports material IDs and references.

Description: Search by material and show related technologies.
Acceptance Criteria: Users can search clay, copper, charcoal, limestone, etc.

Description: Track where raw materials can realistically be found on Earth.
Acceptance Criteria: Materials support regions, deposits, sources, and uncertainty.

Description: Track Moon, Mars, asteroid, habitat, and recycled substitutes.
Acceptance Criteria: Materials show credible space alternatives.

Description: Show sand to glass to lenses to microscopy style chains.
Acceptance Criteria: Atlas can display transformations.

Description: Track in-situ resource utilization relevance.
Acceptance Criteria: Entries support ISRU notes and feasibility.

Description: Indicate whether technology supports recycling and closed-loop habitats.
Acceptance Criteria: Entries show closed-loop relevance.

Description: Support import vs local manufacturing decisions.
Acceptance Criteria: Entries can describe import/local tradeoff.

Description: Track energy intensity where relevant.
Acceptance Criteria: Entries show rough energy class.

Description: Add hazard tags for medical, chemical, electrical, fire, pressure, structural, biological, radiation, sharp tools, high temperature, confined space.
Acceptance Criteria: Schema and validator support hazard categories.

Description: Gate high-risk entries beyond medicine.
Acceptance Criteria: High voltage, chemical, pressure, structural, radiation, and biological entries show appropriate gates.

Description: Rank sources as primary, institutional, textbook, review, encyclopedia, anecdotal.
Acceptance Criteria: Schema and UI show source quality.

Description: Allow critical claims to cite specific sources.
Acceptance Criteria: High-risk entries can cite claims directly.

Description: Mark disputed, weak, or context-dependent knowledge.
Acceptance Criteria: Entries support uncertaintyNotes.

Description: Flag content that changes over time.
Acceptance Criteria: Entries can mark medicine, standards, regulation, electronics as date-sensitive.

Description: Track review events over time.
Acceptance Criteria: Entries can show reviewer, date, scope, and changes.

Description: Group needed reviews by domain.
Acceptance Criteria: Status page shows medical, electrical, civil, chemical, materials, agriculture queues.

Description: Document common myths and dangerous mistakes.
Acceptance Criteria: Entry pages display known bad advice.

Description: Re-rank priorities and notes for Mars.
Acceptance Criteria: Space mode includes Mars-specific constraints.

Description: Add Moon-specific constraints and priorities.
Acceptance Criteria: Scenario mode includes regolith, vacuum, volatiles, temperature cycling.

Description: Focus on closed-loop repair, life support, redundancy, and logistics.
Acceptance Criteria: Scenario mode supports orbital station use case.

Description: Expand first 72 hours, first 30 days, and first year guidance.
Acceptance Criteria: Survive page has phase guides linked to technologies.

Description: Users mark what their group has and see what becomes possible.
Acceptance Criteria: Checklist state feeds recommendations.

Description: Create disease prevention, waste separation, ventilation, food safety guidance.
Acceptance Criteria: Public health chain exists and links core entries.

Description: Create agriculture, storage, seed saving, fermentation, animal husbandry plan.
Acceptance Criteria: Food security chain exists and links entries.

Description: Teach recovery and reuse of parts from ruins.
Acceptance Criteria: Salvage mode lists recoverable materials and safety warnings.

Description: Each mature entry should support at least one diagram.
Acceptance Criteria: Schema and UI support diagram metadata.

Description: Validate filenames, credits, and licenses.
Acceptance Criteria: Validation fails missing media metadata.

Description: Generate printable chains like clean water and low-resource clinic.
Acceptance Criteria: Foundation chains can print cleanly.

Description: Condense key survival content into small cards.
Acceptance Criteria: Selected entries can render field-card view.

Description: Support 1-minute, 5-minute, and deep-dive videos.
Acceptance Criteria: Entry schema/UI supports video categories.

Description: Track which entries need images and sources.
Acceptance Criteria: Status page reports missing media.

Description: Label generated illustrations clearly and prevent treating them as evidence.
Acceptance Criteria: Policy exists and UI can label generated media.

Description: Create templates for factual correction, source improvement, new technology, expert review, and bug.
Acceptance Criteria: .github issue templates exist.

Description: Require source list, validation proof, risk notes, and affected nodes.
Acceptance Criteria: PR template exists.

Description: Define contributor tasks like needs diagram, needs source, needs review.
Acceptance Criteria: Docs or UI lists contribution missions.

Description: Support optional expert reviewer profiles.
Acceptance Criteria: Schema/design exists for reviewer identity.

Description: Plan translations for long-term global usefulness.
Acceptance Criteria: docs includes translation strategy.

Description: Reduce main bundle size by lazy loading routes.
Acceptance Criteria: Build warning reduced or eliminated.

Description: Load index summaries first and full JSON on demand.
Acceptance Criteria: Initial bundle/data weight decreases.

Description: Generate compact static search index at build time.
Acceptance Criteria: Search uses generated index.

Description: Publish JSON Schema for contributor editors.
Acceptance Criteria: Schema file exists and docs link to it.

Description: Make schema evolution safe.
Acceptance Criteria: Scripts migrate older entries.

Description: Automatically verify core pages work offline after installation/cache.
Acceptance Criteria: Test exists or documented check is automated.

Description: Document USB/local network/static mirror workflow.
Acceptance Criteria: docs includes mirror guide.

Description: Plan a static field manual generated from data.
Acceptance Criteria: docs includes book generation plan.
